trade.summary: Japan-Taiwan trade is exceptionally strong and strategically critical, dominated by the semiconductor supply chain. Taiwan supplies Japan with advanced chips (TSMC), and Japan supplies Taiwan with semiconductor equipment, chemicals, and materials. TSMC's decision to build fabs in Japan (Kumamoto, Kyushu — opening 2024; second fab under construction) represents a watershed in bilateral industrial integration. Taiwan runs a significant trade surplus with Japan.
top_products_a_to_b: chemicals and materials for chip fab

diplomatic.summary: Despite the absence of formal diplomatic relations (Japan follows a One China policy), Japan-Taiwan relations are among the closest quasi-diplomatic relationships in the world. The Japan-Taiwan Exchange Association (Japan) and the Taiwan-Japan Relations Association (Taiwan) function as de facto embassies. Japan was among the first to express concern about potential Taiwan Strait crises. Public opinion in Japan is highly favorable toward Taiwan. Former PM Abe Shinzo was particularly pro-Taiwan. Japan's reaction to any Taiwan contingency is the central question in Japan's security planning.
recent_incidents: Chinese PLA military exercises around Taiwan (August 2022, 2023) involved ballistic missiles landing in Japan's EEZ
TSMC Kumamoto fab opening seen as landmark bilateral investment

military.summary: Japan has no formal defense treaty with Taiwan, but Taiwan's defense is inextricably linked to Japan's own security — the Ryukyu Islands (Okinawa) are within 110km of Taiwan. Japan's defense white papers increasingly identify a Taiwan contingency as a direct threat to Japan. Japan's Self-Defense Forces and Taiwan's military maintain informal contacts. Japan's basing of US forces on Okinawa is central to any credible Taiwan defense scenario. Japan has reportedly provided intelligence to Taiwan.
military.alliance_status: Informal alignment — no treaty, strong shared interest
incidents: PLA missiles landing in Japan's EEZ during 2022 exercises created security alarm in Tokyo
Japan's 2022 National Security Strategy explicitly links Taiwan stability to Japan's security

financial.summary: Japan-Taiwan financial ties are deep and growing. TSMC's Kumamoto fab represents a $20+ billion investment with Japanese government subsidies (JASM joint venture with Sony and Denso). Japanese semiconductor materials and equipment companies (Tokyo Electron, Shin-Etsu Chemical, JSR) are deeply integrated with Taiwanese chipmakers. Japanese banks have significant Taiwan operations.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Both Japan and Taiwan are energy importers. Taiwan's CNOOC aspirations in disputed waters create indirect strategic energy linkage. Cooperation on offshore wind energy technology is developing.

scientific.summary: Exceptionally strong technology and scientific collaboration centered on semiconductors. Japanese and Taiwanese semiconductor ecosystems are deeply intertwined. Academic exchanges are robust despite the absence of formal diplomatic relations. Joint research in semiconductor manufacturing, display technology, and materials science is extensive.
